skill.name: youtube-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically extract detailed video metrics and channel information from YouTube based on keyword searches using the BrowserAct API. The Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs such as extract specific keyword YouTube video detailed data, monitor the latest video performance of competitor channels, collect comment and like counts for videos on a specific topic, find AI agent tutorials published this week and extract metrics, evaluate total views and subscriber info for specific videos, scrape detailed metrics of marketing campaign videos, track video trends for a tech topic periodically, get high quality video list data for specified keywords on YouTube, mine detailed information of the latest YouTube videos, collect video duration and engagement data for specific industries, or monitor YouTube content creator performance metrics.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube video metrics and channel data by keyword search through the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Security analysis confirms this skill is safe for publication. All detected patterns are legitimate API integration practices: environment variable access for API credentials (BROWSERACT_API_KEY), network requests to the BrowserAct API service, and script execution for task invocation. No malicious code, data exfiltration, or command injection vulnerabilities were identified.

content.user_title: Extract YouTube Video Metrics by Keyword
content.value_statement: Manually collecting YouTube video metrics is time-consuming and prone to inconsistencies. This skill automates extraction of video titles, view counts, subscriber numbers, and engagement data through the BrowserAct API, providing structured data ready for analysis.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube videos by keyword with date filters
Extract video titles, URLs, and publish dates
Retrieve channel names and subscriber counts
Collect view counts, like counts, and comment counts
Return structured JSON data for downstream processing
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for operation
Does not provide real-time data - depends on BrowserAct task execution time
Rate limits are determined by the BrowserAct service tier

use_cases.title: Track Competitor Channel Performance
use_cases.description: Monitor competitor YouTube channels by extracting their recent video metrics to benchmark your own content performance against theirs.

use_cases.title: Research Video Trends by Topic
use_cases.description: Search for videos on specific topics like AI tutorials or product reviews from the past week to identify trending content patterns and engagement levels.

use_cases.title: Build Video Intelligence Datasets
use_cases.description: Collect structured video metrics for specific keywords to build searchable datasets for BI tools or CRM integrations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube videos about {topic} from this week and show me their titles, views, and subscriber counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple keyword search with date filter

prompt_templates.title: Channel Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract metrics for videos about {topic} published this month, including channel names and engagement data.

prompt_templates.title: Campaign Tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Track YouTube videos about {campaign_name} from the past year. Get view counts, like counts, and comment counts for each.

prompt_templates.title: Weekly Content Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Find top-performing YouTube videos about {industry} from the last 7 days. Extract their titles, URLs, views, and channel information into a table.

best_practices: Always verify your BrowserAct API key is valid before running large-scale extraction tasks
Use specific keywords and narrow date ranges to reduce processing time and API calls
Parse the structured output directly rather than using regex on the text output
anti_patterns: Do not use overly broad keywords like 'video' - results will be noisy and slow
Avoid running multiple concurrent tasks unless you have an appropriate BrowserAct API tier
Do not ignore the 'Invalid authorization' error - an invalid key will cause all retries to fail

faq.question: What is BrowserAct and why do I need an API key?
faq.answer: BrowserAct provides browser automation services for web scraping. The skill uses BrowserAct to extract data from YouTube, and requires an API key from browseract.com to authenticate your requests.

faq.question: How long does a video extraction task take?
faq.answer: Execution time varies based on the number of results and BrowserAct server load. The skill polls for up to 5 minutes and reports status updates every 10 seconds.

faq.question: Can I extract data from private or unlisted videos?
faq.answer: No. The skill only extracts publicly available YouTube video data through search results.

faq.question: What date filters are supported?
faq.answer: Supported filters are: Today, This week, This month, and This year. Use This week as the default for recent content analysis.

faq.question: How many videos can I extract in one request?
faq.answer: The number of results depends on YouTube search results for your keyword. BrowserAct will traverse the results list, but the actual count varies by topic popularity.

faq.question: What should I do if the task times out?
faq.answer: If polling times out after 5 minutes, check your BrowserAct dashboard for task status. Large keyword searches may need to be split into smaller date ranges.
